zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Hammond於1545年7月14日出生在英國素福爾可拉文哈姆時，他的父親John Hammond45歲，母親Agnes Nowell21歲。他於1573年5月14日與Rose Trippe在英國素福爾可拉文哈姆結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1589年11月24日在他的家鄉享年44歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可拉文哈姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Hammond于1545年7月14日出生在英国素福爾可拉文哈姆时，他的父亲John Hammond45岁，母亲Agnes Nowell21岁。他于1573年5月14日与Rose Trippe在英国素福爾可拉文哈姆结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1589年11月24日在他的家乡享年44岁，埋葬在英国英格兰素福爾可拉文哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: Hammond
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English, Old French personal name Ha(i)mon, the oblique case form of the ancient Germanic Ha(i)mo, a short form of various compound names beginning with haim ‘home’. It frequently developed excrescent -d, giving Hamond, Haimund, and Hawmond. Alternatively, the name could derive from the Middle English personal name Hamund (Old Norse Hámundr, composed of the elements hár ‘high’ + mund ‘protection’), which may have been used in Normandy and in 12th-century eastern England, but the former explanation is more likely. The surname was sometimes confused with Almond and Ammon .

English: in the Bradford area of Yorkshire, the name is a shortened form of Ormondroyd, formerly Hamondesrode, from a lost place in Birstall (Yorkshire), named with the Middle English (Old French) personal name Hamon (1 above) + Middle English roid, a southern Yorkshire pronunciation of Old English rod ‘clearing’.
Irish: generally an importation from England, but occasionally an adopted name for Mac Ámoinn, see McCammon .

variants.name: Hammonds
meanings: English (Shropshire and Staffordshire): variant of Hammond , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Almond
meanings: from the Middle English personal name Almund, in which Old English Æthelmund ‘noble protector’ and Ealhmund ‘temple protector’ have coalesced.
variant of   Allman   and   Allmand   both meaning ‘German’, assimilated by folk etymology to the vocabulary word denoting the tree.

variants.name: Hammett
meanings: English (southwestern England, especialy Devon): from the Middle English personal name Hamet, a pet form of ancient Germanic Hamo (see Hammond 1).

variants.name: Hamon
meanings: Breton, French, and English (Channel Islands): from the Breton, Old French, Middle English personal name Hamon, the oblique case form of the ancient Germanic Ha(i)mo, a short form of various compound names beginning with haim ‘home’. See also Hammond .

variants.name: Hamlin
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, from the Norman French and Middle English personal name Hamelin, a double diminutive of Hamo (see Hammond ).
French: variant of   Hamelin   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Hampson
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): patronymic from the Norman personal name Hamo, Hamon (see Hammond ).
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hAmhsaigh ‘descendant of Amhsach’ a byname meaning ‘mercenary soldier’ or ‘messenger’, from the adjective amhasach ‘aggressive’.

variants.name: Haimes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Yacov, Yosef.

English: variant, with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s, of Haime, of Norman origin, from the Old French personal name Haim (ancient Germanic Haimo, from haim- ‘home’), often Latinized as Hamo. Compare Hammond .
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

variants.name: Hammon
meanings: English (Middlesex and Ken): variant of   Hammond  .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Hammond was born on 14 July 1545, in Lavenham, Suffolk, England, his father, John Hammond, was 45 and his mother, Agnes Nowell, was 21. He married Rose Trippe on 14 May 1573, in Lavenham, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 24 November 1589, in his hometown, at the age of 44, and was buried in Lavenham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Hammond est né le 14 juillet 1545, à Lavenham, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, John Hammond, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Agnes Nowell, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Rose Trippe le 14 mai 1573, à Lavenham, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 24 novembre 1589, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterré à Lavenham, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Hammond am 14. Juli 1545 in Lavenham, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Hammond 45 und seine Mutter Agnes Nowell 21. Er hat am 14. Mai 1573 in Lavenham, Suffolk, England Rose Trippe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 24. November 1589 im Alter von 44 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Lavenham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Hammond è nato il il 14 luglio 1545 nel Lavenham, Suffolk, England, il suo padre, John Hammond, aveva 45 anni e il suo madre, Agnes Nowell, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Rose Trippe il 14 maggio 1573, nel Lavenham, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 24 novembre 1589, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 44 anni, e fu sepolto a Lavenham, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Hammondが1545年7月14日にイギリススフォルクラベンハムで生まれた時，父John Hammondは45歳，母Agnes Nowellは21歳であった。1573年5月14日にイギリススフォルクラベンハムでRose Trippeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1589年11月24日に故郷で44歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススフォルクラベンハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Hammond(이)가 1545년 7월 14일 영국 수폴크 레이븐함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Hammond(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Nowell(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1573년 5월 14일 영국 수폴크 레이븐함에서 Rose Trippe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1589년 11월 24일 그의 고향에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 레이븐함에 묻혔다.

pt.14 July 1545–24 November 1589: 14 de julho de 1545–24 de novembro de 1589
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Hammond nasceu em 14 de julho de 1545, em Lavenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Hammond, tinha 45 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Nowell, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Rose Trippe em 14 de maio de 1573, em Lavenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 24 de novembro de 1589, em sua cidade natal, com 44 anos, e foi sepultado em Lavenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Hammond родился 14 июля 1545 г. в местности лавенам, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени John Hammond было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Nowell, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Rose Trippe 14 мая 1573 г. в местности лавенам, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер 24 ноября 1589 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 44 лет и был похоронен в местности лавенам, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.14 July 1545–24 November 1589: 14 de julio de 1545–24 de noviembre de 1589
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Hammond nació el 14 de julio de 1545, en Lavenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, John Hammond, tenía 45 años y su mamá, Agnes Nowell, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Rose Trippe el 14 de mayo de 1573, en Lavenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 24 de noviembre de 1589, en su pueblo, a la edad de 44 años, y fue sepultado en Lavenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
